核心价值观.描述: 塞万提斯一生历经多次重大磨难——列班托海战负伤、阿尔及尔五年囚禁、终生的经济困境——但从未被击垮。他在战场上身患热病仍拒绝躲藏、带伤作战；被俘后四次试图逃亡均告失败，却始终保持尊严与希望；晚年在狱中开始创作《堂吉诃德》。这种在逆境中坚持不屈的精神，是他最显著的品质。
原文引用: Though stricken with a fever, he refused to stay below and joined the thick of the fighting. He received two gunshot wounds in the chest, and a third rendered his left hand useless for the rest of his life. He always looked back on his conduct in the battle with pride. ——Britannica
One man scorned and covered with scars still strove with his last ounce of courage to reach the unreachable stars; and the world will be better for this. ——《堂吉诃德》
During this time of hardship, Cervantes published occasional poems and the idea of writing Don Quixote occurred to him in prison. ——Interlude.hk

核心价值观.描述: 塞万提斯（或其化身堂吉诃德）对理想保持着强烈的信仰与行动热情。尽管现实残酷，堂吉诃德仍执意踏上征途去「扶弱锄强」，这种明知不可为而为之的精神，体现了塞万提斯对理想主义的深刻认同。他相信即使在卑微的位置上，个体也能通过行动改变世界。
原文引用: He takes up his lance and sword to defend the helpless and destroy the wicked, and in the process aids damsels in distress and battles giants, mostly in his own head. ——Interlude.hk
One man scorned and covered with scars still strove with his last ounce of courage to reach the unreachable stars; and the world will be better for this. ——《堂吉诃德》
A good many critics regard Don Quixote as a symbolic, didactic or controversial work intended to bring about radical reforms in church and state. ——Interlude.hk

核心价值观.描述: 尽管文学创作从未为他带来稳定收入（当时的作者没有版权收入），塞万提斯终身笔耕不辍，从未放弃写作。他把在监狱中萌发的《堂吉诃德》构想写成世界第一部现代小说，认为文学本身具有超越个人苦难的价值。他的自我认同首先是文人，而非军人或税收官。
原文引用: During this time of hardship, Cervantes published occasional poems and the idea of writing Don Quixote occurred to him in prison. ——Interlude.hk

Unlike most Spanish writers of his time, including some of humble origin, he apparently did not go to a university. What is certain is that at some stage he became an avid reader of books. ——Britannica

In 1605, Cervantes published the first part of Don Quixote, a novel that tells the story of an elderly man who becomes so enamored by the old stories of brave knights that he seeks out his own adventures. ——Biography.com

核心价值观.描述: 在阿尔及尔被囚期间，塞万提斯因其携带的举荐信而被海盗视为重要人物，但他并未因此遭受酷刑或处死，反而受到相对宽容的对待。他作为俘虏群体中的领袖人物，始终保持尊严。他对堂吉诃德的塑造也体现了对「尊严」的多层探索：理想与现实之间的张力，以及人在荒诞处境中如何保持自尊。
原文引用: He clearly made a name for himself for courage and leadership among the captive community. At long last, in September 1580... Miguel's family, with the aid and intervention of the Trinitarian friars, raised the 500 gold escudos demanded for his release. ——Britannica
His masters, the renegade Dali Mami and later Hasan Paşa, treated him with considerable leniency in the circumstances. ——Britannica
The novel has been critically discussed since the 18th century... as a symbolic, didactic or controversial work. ——Interlude.hk

政治立场.描述: 塞万提斯对西班牙王室持矛盾态度：他服务于皇室军队并引以为荣，但在文学创作中对时弊有含蓄而深刻的批判。《堂吉诃德》表面上是骑士文学的戏仿，实则通过堂吉诃德与桑丘·潘沙的对照，透视了西班牙帝国盛世背后的社会矛盾与精神危机。他并非激进的改革者，也非保守的辩护士，而是以文学讽刺为工具，揭示权力与信仰体制的虚伪，同时对普通人的命运给予深切同情。他的写作具有隐约的伊拉斯谟主义倾向，强调内心道德而非外在仪式。
原文引用: A good many critics regard Don Quixote as a symbolic, didactic or controversial work intended to bring about radical reforms in church and state. It is doubtful, however, that neither contemporaries nor Cervantes himself would have harbored such intentions. ——Interlude.hk

It seems clear that his first intention was merely to parody these extravagances in a short story; but as he proceeded the immense possibilities of the subject became more evident to him, and he ended by expanding his work into a brilliant panorama of Spanish society as it existed during the 16th century. ——Interlude.hk

In 1585, Cervantes published his first novel, La Galatea... Around the same time, Cervantes tried to make it in the then-lucrative world of theater... he achieved neither fortune nor fame with his plays. ——Biography.com

底线清单.描述: 在阿尔及尔被俘期间，面对四次失败的逃亡尝试以及可能遭到处决的风险，塞万提斯始终没有改变宗教信仰或向伊斯兰当局妥协。他将基督教信仰视为身份的核心，即使在最黑暗的囚禁岁月中也未曾动摇。
底线清单.原文引用: He clearly made a name for himself for courage and leadership among the captive community. At long last, in September 1580... Miguel's family, with the aid and intervention of the Trinitarian friars, raised the 500 gold escudos demanded for his release. ——Britannica

底线清单.条目: 不以艺术换取经济依附
底线清单.描述: 尽管文学创作收入微薄，塞万提斯始终保持创作的独立精神，未曾让商业压力彻底支配其艺术方向。《堂吉诃德》并非市场导向的作品；它在狱中诞生，是对骑士传奇的深度反思而非迎合大众口味的消遣。
底线清单.原文引用: Despite its undisputed place in the literary canon, Don Quixote did not make Cervantes wealthy at the time, as authors did not receive royalties for their works. However, he continued to write. ——Biography.com

底线清单.条目: 不放弃对真实与理想的追寻
底线清单.描述: 堂吉诃德最终在死前恢复了理智，但塞万提斯并未因此否定理想主义本身——他否定的是脱离现实的空想，而非理想本身。塞万提斯在作品中始终坚守一个信念：人应当有高于日常的追求，即使这些追求在现实面前显得荒诞。
底线清单.原文引用: The word quixotic, used to mean the impractical pursuit of idealistic goals, entered common usage. The character of Quixote became an archetype. ——Interlude.hk

自我定位.描述: 塞万提斯将自身定位为以笔为剑的文人使命承担者。他的人生经历（从战场到囚牢再到文学）赋予他对社会各阶层的深刻理解，使他能够以「局内人」与「旁观者」的双重身份审视西班牙社会。他视自己为时代的记录者与道德反思者，通过《堂吉诃德》这部「西班牙社会的全景图」揭示人性的普遍困境——即理想与现实的永恒张力。
原文引用: What is certain is that at some stage he became an avid reader of books... he must also, when opportunity offered, have been familiarizing himself with Italian literature. ——Britannica

He ended by expanding his work into a brilliant panorama of Spanish society as it existed during the 16th century. Literary historians consider Don Quixote one of the most important books of all time, and often cite it as the first modern novel. ——Interlude.hk
His work has inspired countless other authors throughout the centuries—including Gustave Flaubert, Henry Fielding and Fyodor Dostoyevsky. ——Biography.com
